Colonoscopy Bowel Prep

{{BAND_LABEL}}{{BAND_LB}}
{{BAND_NOTE}}
Procedure Location
{{LOCATION_NAME}}
{{LOCATION_ADDRESS}}
{{LOCATION_PHONE_LABEL}}: {{LOCATION_PHONE}}
{{LOCATION_ARRIVAL}}

📖 About the Procedure

A colonoscopy is a short procedure done under anesthesia. A pediatric gastroenterologist passes a thin, flexible camera through the bottom to look at the large intestine. Small biopsies are usually taken. The procedure itself takes about 20–45 minutes, but plan to be at the facility for several hours total. The colon must be clean for the camera to see well — that's what the medicine below is for.

✅ Plan Ahead (buy at least 2 days before)

Pick up these items at least 2 days before the procedure.

Active ingredient / ItemWhat to get
CLENPIQ (sodium picosulfate, magnesium oxide, and citric acid) — prescription required
Call the office if you have not received a prescription. CLENPIQ is ready to drink — no mixing needed.		 {{HTML_CLENPIQ_TOTAL_BOTTLES}} bottles
{{HTML_CLENPIQ_BOTTLE_OZ}} oz / {{HTML_CLENPIQ_BOTTLE_ML}} mL each
Clear liquids — water, Gatorade or Powerade (any color except red or purple), apple juice, white grape juice At least 72 oz (~2 L) total. Include a balanced electrolyte drink (Gatorade, Pedialyte, or similar).
Skin protection — protective ointment (Desitin or Vaseline) for anal-area comfort 1 tube
⚠️ CLENPIQ already contains a stimulant laxative (sodium picosulfate). Do NOT give Dulcolax (bisacodyl) or any other laxative with this prep unless your doctor specifically tells you to.
⚠️ 💊 Medications

Prepare a list of medications and be ready to discuss them with the nurse or scheduler when they call.
Stop {{HTML_MEDICATIONS_DRUGS}} 7 days before.
If taking tetracycline or fluoroquinolone antibiotics, iron, digoxin, chlorpromazine, or penicillamine — take these at least 2 hours before and not less than 6 hours after each CLENPIQ dose.
Give all other oral medications at least 1 hour before starting each CLENPIQ dose.
You can verify what medications may need to be stopped at meds.giready.com.

{{HTML_PRECLEANOUT_BLOCK}}

📅 3 Days Before — Low-Residue Diet

AVOID OK to eat
Whole grains, nuts, seeds White rice, pasta, potatoes (no skin)
Raw fruits and vegetables Bananas, melons, cooked veggies
Corn, beans, popcorn Eggs, chicken, fish, yogurt, pudding

📅 1 Day Before — Clear Liquids All Day

Clear liquids only — no solid food, no dairy for the entire day.

AVOID OK to drink
ALL solid food and dairy Water, Gatorade/Powerade (not red/purple)
RED or PURPLE drinks or foods Apple juice, white grape juice
Alcohol; juices with pulp Lemonade, clear soda, clear broth; popsicles, plain Jell-O (not red/purple); tea/coffee without milk

🥤 DOSE 1 — Evening Before the Procedure

Best to refrigerate the bottles ahead of time — CLENPIQ tastes better cold.

Between {{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE1_WINDOW}}
Drink 1 full bottle ({{HTML_CLENPIQ_BOTTLE_OZ}} oz / {{HTML_CLENPIQ_BOTTLE_ML}} mL) of CLENPIQ. It is cranberry-flavored and ready to drink — no mixing needed.
After the dose
Drink at least {{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE1_CLEARS_CUPS}} × {{HTML_DRINK_CUP}} cups ({{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE1_CLEARS_OZ}} oz total) of clear liquids over the next {{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE1_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours. Include Gatorade, Pedialyte, or another electrolyte drink among them.
💡 Tips
  • Refrigerate the bottles ahead of time — CLENPIQ tastes better cold.
  • Drink each cup of clear liquid steadily — don't wait until the end.
  • Expect diarrhea to start within 1–3 hours — this is normal and means the prep is working.
  • Apply protective ointment to the bottom area before diarrhea starts.
  • If severe bloating, distension, or abdominal pain occurs after Dose 1, delay Dose 2 until symptoms resolve.

🥤 DOSE 2 — Morning of the Procedure

{{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE2_HOURS_BEFORE_MIN}}–{{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE2_HOURS_BEFORE_MAX}} hours before procedure
Drink 1 full bottle ({{HTML_CLENPIQ_BOTTLE_OZ}} oz / {{HTML_CLENPIQ_BOTTLE_ML}} mL) of CLENPIQ.
After the dose
Drink at least {{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE2_CLEARS_CUPS}} × {{HTML_DRINK_CUP}} cups ({{HTML_CLENPIQ_DOSE2_CLEARS_OZ}} oz total) of clear liquids, finishing at least {{NPO_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours before the procedure.

⚠️ Stop all clear liquids {{NPO_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours before the procedure. If your child vomits within 30 minutes of a dose, wait 30 minutes and try giving the dose again slowly.

📅 Day of Procedure

  • Clear liquids only. No solid food.
  • Stop all clear liquids {{NPO_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours before the procedure.
  • Bring comfort items for your child (toy, blanket, tablet, etc.).
⚠️ If the prep is not followed exactly, the colonoscopy may be delayed or canceled.

Helpful Resources

▶ Watch a video about the procedure Example only — follow this handout, not the video. 📨 Patient portal Message us — preferred contact method 🌐 GIKids.org General info on pediatric GI procedures

🍚 Low-Residue Diet — Sample Meals

Meal ideas for the low-residue (“white”) diet. Continue this diet for the 3 days before the procedure. The day before the procedure, switch to clear liquids only — no dairy.

Breakfast (morning)

  • Scrambled eggs
  • White toast with butter
  • Banana
  • Yogurt (no seeds or berries)
  • Apple juice

Lunch (~noon)

  • Plain chicken sandwich on white bread (no veggies)
  • Mac and cheese
  • White rice with chicken
  • Plain pasta
  • Applesauce
  • Vanilla pudding

After lunch (~2:00 PM onward)

  • Clear liquids only (see allowed drinks above)
  • No more dairy — milk, yogurt, ice cream, etc.
After the procedure & when to call
  • Your child will wake up in the recovery area. Some grogginess and gas/cramping/bloating are normal for several hours.
  • A small amount of blood with the first stool after a colonoscopy can be normal, especially if biopsies were taken.
  • An adult must stay with your child for 24 hours after anesthesia.
  • Start with clear liquids, then advance to a regular diet as tolerated.
  • No school, daycare, or sports for the rest of the day. Most children are back to normal activity the next day.
  • The doctor will discuss what was seen before you go home. Biopsy results take about 1–2 weeks; we'll send the results to the portal and discuss over the phone or at the next appointment.

Call (317) 338-9450 if your child has any of the following after the procedure:

  • A temperature over 101°F (38.3°C)
  • Stool that is black or has more than a small streak of blood
  • Severe or worsening abdominal pain, or a hard, swollen belly
  • Persistent vomiting, or refusal to drink
  • Difficulty breathing or chest pain — this is an emergency, call 911
Pediatric Gastroenterology
Office: (317) 338-9450
